questions.question: What is a Copy Constructor in C++?
options: A) A constructor that creates a copy of an object
B) A constructor that initializes an object
C) A destructor that frees resources
D) A copy assignment operator
questions.explanation: A Copy Constructor is used to create a copy of an existing object. It is called when you pass an object to a function by value or when you assign one object to another.

questions.question: What happens when you pass an object to a function by value in C++?
options: A) The object is copied
B) The object is moved
C) The object is passed by reference
D) The object is passed by const pointer
questions.explanation: When you pass an object to a function by value, a copy constructor is called to create a copy of the object.

questions.question: Can you create a copy of an object using the assignment operator?
options: A) Yes, using the copy assignment operator
B) No, using the assignment operator
C) Yes, using the move assignment operator
D) Only using the copy constructor
questions.explanation: Yes, you can create a copy of an object using the assignment operator. The assignment operator is often referred to as the copy assignment operator.

questions.question: Why would you use a move constructor?
options: A) To create a copy of an object
B) To move an object into a new location
C) To assign one object to another
D) To create a copy of an object using the assignment operator
questions.explanation: You would use a move constructor when you need to transfer ownership of an object from one location to another, without copying the object.

questions.question: What is the difference between a copy constructor and a move constructor?
options: A) A copy constructor creates a copy, while a move constructor transfers ownership
B) A copy constructor transfers ownership, while a move constructor creates a copy
C) A copy constructor is used for assignment, while a move constructor is used for passing by value
D) A copy constructor is used for passing by reference, while a move constructor is used for passing by value
questions.explanation: A copy constructor creates a copy of an object, while a move constructor transfers ownership of an object from one location to another, without copying the object.
